projects.title: AI-Powered Internal Support Ticket Resolution System
projects.description: LLM-based ticket automation resolving 45% of IT tickets without human intervention. React portal + FastAPI + Claude API.
projects.longDescription: Built an AI-powered internal support system resolving 45% of IT tickets automatically. Frontend: React-based portal with real-time chat, SSO (Active Directory) integration. Backend: Python FastAPI orchestrating Claude API for intent classification, OpenSearch knowledge base retrieval, and automated responses. AI Pipeline: Multi-step workflow classifying tickets, retrieving documentation, generating solutions with confidence scores. Auto-Resolution: High-confidence tickets (>85%) resolved automatically. Infrastructure: AWS Lambda, SQS, RDS (MySQL), S3. Outcome: 45% auto-resolution, resolution time reduced from 4 hours to 12 minutes.
impact: Resolution time reduced from 4 hours to 12 minutes

projects.title: Intelligent Document Processing & Compliance Assistant
projects.description: GPT-4/Claude-based compliance document processing with 99.2% accuracy. Reduced manual review by 70%.
projects.longDescription: Built intelligent document processing system for compliance team manually reviewing trade documents across 15 countries. Frontend: React dashboard with drag-drop upload, real-time processing status. Backend: Java Spring Boot microservices with Python LLM processing, JWT-authenticated APIs. AI Pipeline: GPT-4 for document understanding, entity extraction (counterparties, amounts, dates), Claude API for compliance rule matching. Integration: Webhooks to Salesforce CRM, EventBridge for scheduled reports. Infrastructure: S3, Lambda, CloudWatch, Route 53, CloudFront. Outcome: 70% reduction in manual review, accuracy improved from 92% to 99.2%.
impact: 70% reduction in manual document review time

projects.title: AI Sales Outreach & Pipeline Automation
projects.description: LLM-powered sales automation with personalized email generation. 3x pipeline increase, 23% response rate.
projects.longDescription: Built AI sales outreach automation for manual email crafting and prospect tracking. Frontend: React dashboard with pipeline stages, prospect scores, AI-generated email drafts, WebSocket real-time notifications. Backend: Node.js/Express with Python ML services, MySQL, Redis caching. AI Pipeline: Lead Scoring via LLM analyzing company data and LinkedIn profiles; Email Generation via Claude API for personalized sequences; Response Analysis classifying replies and triggering workflows. Integrations: HubSpot CRM sync, Gmail API, Slack webhooks. Infrastructure: Docker on EKS, GitHub Actions CI/CD, CloudWatch, OpenSearch. Outcome: 3x qualified pipeline increase, response rate improved from 8% to 23%.
impact: Automated lead scoring and email personalization
Real-time Slack notifications for sales team

Zero-downtime migration with comprehensive rollback strategies.

blog.title: Scaling Payments to 1 Million Users: The Complete Architecture
blog.excerpt: Comprehensive system design guide for high-throughput payment architectures with load balancing, connection pooling, and queue-based processing.
blog.content: Complete system design guide for payment services handling 1 million concurrent requests. Covers ACID guarantees at scale, idempotency keys with Redis, database connection pooling with HikariCP, tiered queue systems for priority processing, and circuit breakers with Resilience4j. Includes monitoring strategies, backpressure implementation, and optimistic locking patterns. The final architecture achieves 99.99% availability with sub-100ms p99 latency.

blog.title: Mastering MySQL Concurrency: Isolation Levels & Locks
blog.excerpt: Understanding MySQL's Multi-Version Concurrency Control, locking mechanisms, and transaction isolation levels with practical examples.
blog.content: Deep dive into MySQL concurrency covering MVCC (Multi-Version Concurrency Control), four isolation levels (Read Uncommitted, Read Committed, Repeatable Read, Serializable), and concurrency problems (dirty reads, non-repeatable reads, phantom reads). Explains lock types including Shared Locks, Exclusive Locks, Gap Locks, and Next-Key Locks. Includes deadlock prevention strategies, monitoring queries (SHOW ENGINE INNODB STATUS), and best practices for consistent table access order.

blog.title: Database Locks Unlocked: A Practical Guide
blog.excerpt: Complete guide to database locking mechanisms with practical implementation examples for MySQL and MSSQL.
blog.content: Comprehensive guide to database locking strategies. Shared Locks allow multiple readers but block writers—ideal for financial reports. Exclusive Locks block all access—used by UPDATE/DELETE/INSERT. Pessimistic Locking prevents conflicts upfront with SELECT FOR UPDATE for high-contention scenarios. Optimistic Locking with version numbers suits read-heavy systems. Includes comparison table for banking (Pessimistic + Serializable), e-commerce (Pessimistic with short transactions), social media (Optimistic), and analytics (Shared/MVCC). Covers common pitfalls like lock escalation and deadlock prevention.

blog.title: Kafka: The Complete Playbook for Developers
blog.excerpt: 53-page comprehensive guide covering Kafka fundamentals, architecture, cluster setup, Producer/Consumer APIs, streaming, and production best practices.
blog.content: Complete 53-page developer guide to Apache Kafka. Core concepts: Topics as logical channels, Partitions for parallelism, Producers with partition keys, Consumers in groups. Configuration: acks=all for durability, retries=3, batch.size for throughput, enable.auto.commit=false. Patterns: Event Sourcing, CQRS, Dead Letter Queues for poison pills, Exactly-Once Semantics with idempotent producers. Includes cluster setup, Producer/Consumer API examples, Kafka Streams basics, and monitoring essentials (Consumer Lag, Under-Replicated Partitions, Request Latency).

blog.title: ProxySQL: Supercharge Your MySQL Performance
blog.excerpt: Complete guide to ProxySQL including query routing, connection pooling, load balancing, and production optimization strategies.
blog.content: Complete guide to ProxySQL MySQL proxy covering Connection Pooling to reduce MySQL connection overhead, Query Routing for automatic read/write splitting, Query Caching for microsecond response times, and Query Rewriting to modify queries without code changes. Configuration includes Hostgroups for writers/readers, Query Rules for routing SELECTs to replicas, and connection pool tuning. Advanced features: Query Mirroring for testing, Query Throttling for expensive queries, Automatic Failover. Real results: connections reduced from 10,000 to 100, p99 latency from 50ms to 5ms.

blog.title: Linear Regression Deep Dive: Math to Implementation
blog.excerpt: Mathematical foundation of MLR including loss function, gradient descent derivation, and practical Python implementation.
blog.content: Mathematical derivation of Multiple Linear Regression with y = beta0 + beta1x1 + beta2x2 + ... + betanxn + error. Covers problem setup, loss function (MSE), matrix expressions, gradient derivation, and normal equation. Key assumptions: Linearity, Independence, Homoscedasticity, Normality, No Multicollinearity (VIF scores). Coefficient interpretation with practical examples. Evaluation metrics: R-squared, Adjusted R-squared, RMSE. Implementation with Python statsmodels and scikit-learn. Common pitfalls: Overfitting, Outliers, Omitted Variable Bias, Data Leakage, Extrapolation.

blog.title: SSL/TLS From Zero to Hero: Complete Security Guide
blog.excerpt: Comprehensive guide to SSL/TLS protocols, x509 certificates, handshake process, and certificate installation procedures.
blog.content: Complete guide to SSL/TLS covering Cryptography Fundamentals (symmetric/asymmetric encryption, hashing), x509 Certificates and Keys (CSR generation, CA chains, certificate formats), Handshake Process (TLS 1.2 vs 1.3, cipher suites, key exchange), and Certificate Installation (Apache, Nginx, Load Balancers). Includes troubleshooting common issues, certificate renewal automation, and security best practices for HTTPS implementation.

recommendations.name: Subrahmanyam Ch
recommendations.text: I had the privilege of working with Vinayak Chiluka, an exceptional software engineer and leader with over a decade of experience. He has deep expertise across various technology stacks, including Java, Python, C++, and AWS, and consistently delivers high-quality solutions even in demanding environments. Vinayak is a true team player, leading with grace and efficiency while mentoring others and successfully managing complex projects. His work in IoT, financial trading, and eCommerce highlights his versatility and strategic thinking, particularly in optimizing resources for high-traffic systems. Vinayak's positive attitude, collaborative spirit, and passion for continuous learning, especially in AI and machine learning, make him a valuable asset. I highly recommend him for any role that demands technical excellence and leadership.

recommendations.name: Firoz Alam
recommendations.text: I would like to recommend Vinayak for the below skills as he worked well on these tech stacks: 1. DSA 2. AWS Certified 3. System Design 4. Java. He has excellent problem solving skills with good grasping power.

recommendations.name: Sandeep Kumar Jakkaraju
recommendations.text: I had the pleasure of working with Vinayak Chiluka on the ABB project, and I cannot recommend him highly enough. Vinayak consistently demonstrated exceptional technical skills, particularly in the areas we focused on. His ability to solve complex problems and deliver high-quality solutions was evident throughout the project. Vinayak's deep understanding of the technology stack and his proactive approach to challenges made a significant impact on our project's success. His work on the initial CogniEN project was equally impressive, where he brought innovative ideas to the table and executed them flawlessly. Beyond his technical expertise, Vinayak is a team player with excellent communication skills. He collaborates effectively with colleagues and is always willing to go the extra mile to ensure the project's success. Vinayak is a true asset to any team, and I am confident that he will continue to excel in all his future endeavours.

recommendations.name: Aashish Bhagat
recommendations.text: I had the pleasure of working with Vinayak on the same team at Amazon, where he served as a Software Engineer II. He has a deep understanding of AWS services and consistently demonstrated his ability to guide and mentor new hires effectively. His strong problem-solving skills allow him to tackle complex issues and provide well-thought-out solutions. What sets Vinayak apart is his focus on simplification and innovation. He constantly seeks ways to improve processes and invent new solutions that enhance efficiency. Additionally, he possesses excellent communication skills, enabling him to convey requirements clearly and effectively to leadership. Overall, he is a highly skilled and reliable engineer who would be an asset to any team.

recommendations.name: Sindhu Nagaraj
recommendations.text: I have collaborated with Vinayak over a year to work on AWS Cloud application Development, he is exceptional in building secure cloud applications and his depth of concept understanding is amazing. It's been a pleasure working with him.
